task: Direct follow-up: 'sql executed now lets build the signin.' js/themeRepositoryClient.js gained signIn(email,password)/signOut()/getIdentity() alongside the existing, unchanged anonymous _ensureAuth() -- signing in is an upgrade, never a requirement, so every session still authors normally with zero action. Signing in REPLACES the active session (RLS-scoped by auth.uid()), a disclosed consequence, not a bug -- content owned by the prior anonymous identity becomes invisible, since this is a real sign-in, not Supabase's separate anonymous-session-conversion mechanism. World Builder's Welcome screen gained a small #wb-identity-bar (four honest states matching _annotateProjectBadges's own diagnostic pattern) and a Sign In modal reusing the existing standalone modal shell and field CSS. A real boot-wiring bug was caught and fixed during Playwright verification: boot never called showWelcome(), only renderMyWorlds() directly, so the identity bar rendered blank until wired into that same boot call. Verified via a stateful mock Repository client (anonymous to open-modal to wrong-password-shows-inline-error to correct-password-closes-and-updates-bar to sign-out-reverts), zero console errors; full regression across every prior World Builder script and goldenBuild.js's 30-assertion suite passes unchanged. No RLS/schema change needed. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
